prompt: Photorealistic cinematic illustration of a colossal, ancient library disappearing into heavy shadows. Ninety-nine percent of the massive wooden shelves are completely bare, covered in thick grey dust and cobwebs, rotting away. In the lower right corner, on a single intact shelf, one small ancient book lies open, illuminated by the fragile, warm amber light of a solitary flickering candle. The vast emptiness of the ruined shelves dominates the composition, pressing down on the single point of light. Mood: profound silence, tragic irreversible loss, fragile hope. Color palette: heavy charcoal grey, cold sepia, and a single point of warm ochre. Wide angle empty space. No text, no words, no labels, no numbers, no chart elements visible anywhere.
insight: A language dies every 2 weeks — and with it, an irreplaceable way of seeing the world.
